properties.title: Properties
properties.selectOperation: Select an operation to view properties

tooltips.boardSize: The calculated total dimensions of all loaded files.
tooltips.originOffset: Preview a new X/Y origin. Click 'Apply Origin' to save this position as the new (0,0).
tooltips.boardRotation: Apply a rotation (in degrees) to all operations around the current origin.
tooltips.center: Preview the origin at the center of the board's bounding box.
tooltips.bottomLeft: Preview the origin at the bottom-left corner of the board's bounding box.
tooltips.applyOrigin: Saves the current previewed offset as the new permanent origin (0,0) for the project.
tooltips.resetUnsaved: Discards any unsaved origin offset changes and reverts to the last saved origin.
tooltips.applyRotation: Applies the specified rotation to all geometry.
tooltips.resetRotation: Resets the board rotation to 0 without changing the origin position.

tooltips.pcbThickness: The total thickness of your PCB material (e.g., 1.6mm). Used for cutout depth calculations.
tooltips.safeZ: Maximum safe height for rapid moves and tool changes (G0). Must clear all clamps and fixtures.
tooltips.travelZ: Height above the board (Z=0) where the tool travels between cutting paths.
tooltips.rapidFeed: The feed rate (mm/min) for G0 rapid movements (travel and safe Z moves).
tooltips.postProcessor: Selects the G-code dialect for your specific CNC controller (e.g., GRBL, Marlin).
tooltips.gcodeUnits: Sets the units for the exported G-code file (G21 for mm, G20 for inches).

tooltips.grid: Toggle the visibility of the canvas grid.
tooltips.wireframe: Render all geometry as thin outlines instead of filled shapes.
tooltips.boardBounds: Show a red box representing the total size of all loaded files.
tooltips.fusionMode: Merge all overlapping isolation/clearing geometry into a single object. Required for Arc Reconstruction.
tooltips.preprocessed: Shows the geometry *after* fusion but *before* arc reconstruction.
tooltips.arcReconstruction: Attempt to rebuild arcs from line segments in fused geometry. Requires Fusion Mode.
tooltips.verboseDebug: Show 'debug' level messages in the status log panel.

comments.toolChangePause: Tool change pause - press cycle start

makera.mtcRelease: Paused. Press Play to release collet.
makera.mtcInsert: Paused. Insert tool and press Play to close.

status.default: Ready - Add PCB files to begin
status.logHint: Click here to expand log panel
status.logHintViz: Toggle verbose debug messages in the Viz Panel

messages.noOperations: No operations loaded. Add PCB files to begin.
messages.noOpsReady: No operations ready. Generate previews first.
messages.noGcodeToExport: No G-code to export. Click 'Calculate Toolpaths' first.
messages.opsMissingPreview: Operations missing Preview: {names}. Please generate previews first.

welcome.title: Welcome to EasyTrace5000
welcome.subtitle: Advanced isolation routing for PCB manufacturing
welcome.exampleHelp: Select an example to explore functionality.

exporter.title: Toolpath Manager & Export
exporter.orderHelp: Drag operations to reorder. Operations will be processed in this sequence.

tooltips.order: Drag and drop operations to set the final cutting order.
tooltips.exportSingle: Combine all selected operations into one G-code file. If unchecked, you will get one file per operation.
tooltips.includeComments: Add human-readable comments (e.g., '; Starting Operation: isolation.gbr') to the G-code.
tooltips.toolChanges: Insert a pause (M0 or M6) when the tool changes between operations. (Not yet implemented)
tooltips.optimize: Applies multiple algorithms to reduce operation time. Disable to try and remove unexpected toolpaths.

parameters.tool: Select a tool from your library. The tool's diameter will be used for calculations.
parameters.toolDiameter: The physical diameter of the cutting tool. This is the most critical value for accurate offsets.
parameters.passes: Number of offset passes to create. More passes clear more copper but take longer.
parameters.stepOver: Percentage of the tool diameter to overlap on each pass. 50% is a good default.
parameters.combineOffsets: Merge all offset passes into a single geometry. Reduces G-code complexity but loses individual pass control.
parameters.millHoles: If checked, generates circular milling paths inside any drill holes larger than the tool. If unchecked, generates a simple peck/drill at the center.
parameters.cutSide: Which side of the cutout line to place the tool. 'Outside' is standard for cutting a board out.
parameters.cutDepth: Final target depth for all cuts, relative to the material surface (Z=0). Should be a negative value (e.g., -0.05mm).
parameters.depthPerPass: Incremental depth of each pass when using multi-depth cutting. Smaller = gentler on tool.
parameters.multiDepth: Enable multiple passes at different depths for deep cuts. Reduces tool stress.
parameters.feedRate: Speed at which the tool moves while cutting (mm/min or in/min).
parameters.plungeRate: Speed at which the tool descends into material (mm/min or in/min). Usually slower than feed rate.
parameters.spindleSpeed: Rotation speed of the spindle (RPM). Higher speeds for smaller tools.
parameters.spindleDwell: Time in seconds to wait. Essential for high inertia spindles that need extra time to come up to speed.
parameters.direction: Climb milling: tool rotation matches feed direction (better finish). Conventional: opposite direction (more stable).
parameters.entryType: How the tool enters the material. Plunge: straight down. Ramp: diagonal. Helix: spiral down.
parameters.cannedCycle: Pre-programmed drill cycle. G83 (peck drilling) retracts periodically to clear chips.
parameters.peckDepth: Depth to drill before retracting when using peck drilling. Smaller = more retracts = cleaner holes.
parameters.dwellTime: Pause duration at bottom of hole (seconds). Allows chips to clear and reduces burrs.
parameters.retractHeight: Height to retract to between holes. Should clear board but minimize travel time.
parameters.tabs: Number of small tabs to leave uncut, holding the board in place.
parameters.tabWidth: The length of each holding tab in mm.
parameters.tabHeight: The thickness of the tab. A value of -0.5mm on a -1.8mm cut will leave a 0.5mm thick tab.
parameters.startCode: Custom Preamble to add to the beginning of this operation's command stream.
parameters.endCode: Custom Postamble to add to the end of this operation's command stream.
parameters.laserSpotSize: The effective diameter of the laser beam on the material surface (kerf width). This is a machine property — change it in Machine Settings. All offset calculations use this value as the equivalent of a tool diameter.
parameters.laserIsolationWidth: Total width of copper to ablate around traces, measured from the trace edge outward. The number of laser passes is automatically calculated from this value, the spot size, and the step-over percentage.
parameters.laserStepOver: Percentage of the spot diameter that adjacent paths overlap. Lower values (more overlap) give better copper removal but increase processing time. 50% is a good starting point; reduce to 30% if streaks appear.
parameters.laserClearStrategy:
How the laser fills areas that need copper removal.

• Filled Polygon: Exports solid shapes — your laser software handles the fill pattern. SVG only.
• Offset Paths: Concentric paths shrinking inward. Most reliable for streak prevention.
• Parallel Hatch: Straight lines at a fixed angle. Fast but single-direction.
• Cross-Hatch: Two perpendicular sets of lines. Best coverage, longest processing time.
parameters.laserHatchAngle: Angle of the hatch lines in degrees. For parallel hatch, lines run at this angle. For cross-hatch, the second set runs perpendicular (angle + 90°). 45° is a good default — it crosses both horizontal and vertical traces.
parameters.laserCutSide:
Where the laser kerf falls relative to the board outline.

• Outside: Laser cuts outside the line (board stays full size).
• Inside: Laser cuts inside the line (board shrinks by kerf).
• On Line: Laser follows the exact line (board shrinks by half the kerf on each side).
parameters.stencilOffset: Shrinks (negative) or expands (positive) the stencil apertures. -0.05mm is a common default to prevent solder bridging.
parameters.stencilIgnoreRegions: If checked, ignores large copper pours and traces, only generating apertures for actual component pads (flashes).
parameters.stencilExcludeDrillPads: Automatically removes stencil apertures that sit directly over through-holes to prevent solder paste from falling into the holes.
parameters.stencilAddRegHoles: Generates 4 circular alignment holes outside the board boundaries to help pin the stencil in place during manual application.
parameters.stencilRegDiameter: The physical diameter of the registration pins/dowels you plan to use.
parameters.stencilRegMargin: How far outside the calculated board boundary to place the registration holes.

originControls.originOffset: Set a new X/Y origin. Click 'Apply Origin' to save this position as the new (0,0).
originControls.boardRotation: Apply a rotation (in degrees) to all files around itself.
originControls.mirrorGeometry: Invert file geometry. Usefull with bottom-layer isolation.

machineSettings.pcbThickness: The total thickness of your PCB material (e.g., 1.6mm). Used for cutout depth calculations.
machineSettings.safeZ: Maximum safe height for rapid moves and tool changes (G0). Must clear all clamps and fixtures.
machineSettings.travelZ: Height above the board (Z=0) where the tool travels between cutting paths.
machineSettings.rapidFeed: The feed rate (mm/min) for G0 rapid movements (travel and safe Z moves).
machineSettings.postProcessor: Selects the G-code dialect for your specific CNC controller (Non-Grbl modules need more testing, use with caution).
machineSettings.gcodeUnits: Sets the units for the exported G-code file (G21 for mm, G20 for inches).
machineSettings.rolandMachineModel: Select your Roland machine to auto-populate common settings (steps/mm, feed limits, Z mode). All fields remain editable after selection.
machineSettings.rolandStepsPerMM: Motor steps per millimeter of travel. Determines coordinate resolution. MDX-50/40A: 100, MDX-15/20: 40. Incorrect values scale all coordinates proportionally.
machineSettings.rolandZMode: 3D mode uses the Z command for simultaneous XYZ interpolation (MDX-40A and above). 2.5D mode uses PU/PD which only supports XY cutting at a fixed depth set by !PZ.
machineSettings.rolandMaxFeed: Maximum XY feed rate in mm/sec. The VS (velocity) command is clamped to this value. Exceeding it may cause the machine to reject or ignore commands.
machineSettings.rolandSpindleMode: 'Direct RPM' sends explicit speed values via !RC. 'Indexed' sends a preset 1-15 via !RC. 'Manual Only' means the machine has a fixed speed — software only controls on/off via !MC.
machineSettings.rolandSpindleSpeed: Target spindle speed sent via the !RC command. Only applies in 'Direct RPM' mode.
machineSettings.rolandSpindleIndex: Speed preset index (1 = slowest, 15 = fastest) sent via !RC. Only applies in 'Indexed' mode.
machineSettings.laserSpotSize: The effective cut width (kerf) of your laser beam on copper. Measure by making a single test line and measuring the actual ablated width. Typical values: UV laser 0.02-0.08mm, Fiber laser 0.03-0.15mm, CO2 laser 0.1-0.3mm.
machineSettings.laserExportFormat: SVG exports vector paths that laser software (LightBurn, RDWorks) interprets with its own power/speed settings per color layer. PNG exports a raster image at the specified DPI — useful for direct image engraving modes.
machineSettings.laserExportDPI: Resolution of the exported PNG image. Higher DPI gives finer detail but larger file size. 1000 DPI is suitable for most PCB work. Match this to your laser software's engraving resolution setting.
machineSettings.laserClearStrategy: Default clearing strategy for new operations. Can be overridden per-operation in the parameter panel. Offset Paths is recommended as a safe default — it guarantees overlap between passes without relying on external software.
machineSettings.stencilExportPadding: Physical margin (in mm) added around the stencil geometry in the exported SVG. Provides breathing room for alignment on vinyl cutters and laser plotters.

vizPanel.grid: Toggle the visibility of the canvas grid.
vizPanel.wireframe: Render all geometry as thin outlines instead of filled shapes.
vizPanel.boardBounds: Show a box representing the total size of all loaded files.
vizPanel.rulers: Show/hide the rulers around the canvas.
vizPanel.offsets: Show/hide the calculated offset geometry. This is automatically hidden when a Preview is generated.
vizPanel.previews: Show/hide the toolpath previews, which represent the actual path of the tool.
vizPanel.fusionMode: Merge all overlapping isolation/clearing geometry. Required for Arc Reconstruction.
vizPanel.preprocessed: Shows the geometry *after* fusion but *before* arc reconstruction. Requires Fusion Mode.
vizPanel.arcReconstruction: Attempt to rebuild arcs from line segments in fused geometry. Requires Fusion Mode.
vizPanel.debugPoints: Show all individual points in all geometry.
vizPanel.bwMode: Render the canvas in black and white, ignoring layer colors.
vizPanel.verboseDebug: Show 'debug' level messages in the status log panel. Requires a page reload to see startup messages.

exporter.order: Drag and drop operations to set the final cutting order.
exporter.postProcessor: Overrides the global machine post-processor for this export only.
exporter.singleFile: Combine all selected operations into one G-code file. If unchecked, you will get one file per operation.
exporter.splitDrills: When enabled, peck drill operations are separated into individual files grouped by original hole diameter. Milled holes are exported together in their own file. Requires multi-file export mode.
exporter.includeComments: Add human-readable comments (e.g., '; Starting Operation: isolation.gbr') to the G-code.
exporter.toolChanges: Insert a pause (M0 or M6) when the tool changes between operations. (Not yet implemented)
exporter.optimize: Applies multiple algorithms to reduce operation time. Disable to try and remove unexpected toolpaths.
exporter.layerColors: Assign distinct colors to each operation type. Your laser software uses these colors to apply different power/speed settings per layer.
exporter.heatManagement: Adds spatial scattering to the default smallest-first ordering. Features of similar size are distributed across the board using a grid-hopping pattern that maximizes distance between consecutive cuts. Area sorting (smallest features first within each pass) is always active regardless of this toggle. Your laser software must process paths in file order — disable 'Optimize Cut Path' in LightBurn or equivalent in RDWorks.
exporter.reverseCutOrder: Reverses the cutting order so the largest features are processed first. Use this if your laser software reads SVG paths bottom-to-top (last element first) instead of the standard top-to-bottom DOM order.
exporter.filename: The name of the G-code file that will be downloaded.
exporter.calculate: Process the selected operations and generate the G-code preview based on the current order and settings.
